Leenhouts, PW. 1982. Loganiaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 9: 567-568

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 10 Juni 2026

species.citation: J.R.Forst. & G.Forst. , Char. Gen. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1776) 24 ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 371; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 11. = Geniostoma batanense Merr. , PJS 3c (1908) 427 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 309. = Geniostoma brevipes Merr. , PJS 9c (1914) 384 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 309. = Geniostoma cumingianum Benth. , J. Proc. Linn. Soc., Bot. 1 (1857) 97 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310. = Geniostoma fasciculatum Quisumb. & Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1928) 190, as 'fasciculata'. = Geniostoma laxum Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2746 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310. = Geniostoma lancilimbum Merr. , PJS 17 (1920) 304 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310. = Geniostoma longipes Merr. , PJS 12c (1917) 296 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310. = Geniostoma mindanaense Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2747. = Geniostoma nigrescens (Blanco) Merr. , Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 305 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310; ≡ Tayotum nigrescens Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 105. = Geniostoma pachyphyllum Merr. , PJS 14 (1919) 448 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310. = Geniostoma philippinense Merr. , PJS 3c (1908) 259. = Geniostoma pulgarense Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1808 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310. = Geniostoma ramosii Merr. , PJS 17 (1920) 304 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310. = Geniostoma stenophyllum Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 329 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 310. var. rupestre. Distribution: Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sumatra, Taiwan. BATAN, BATANES, BILIRAN, CATANDUANES, CEBU (photos), HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Benguet, Camarines, Cavite, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao Oriental (photos), Surigao del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SIARGAO, SIBUYAN. Native.
species.distribution: Australia, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan. BATAN, BATANES, BILIRAN, CATANDUANES, CEBU (photos), HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Apayao (photos), Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Benguet, Camarines, Cavite, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao de Oro (photos), Surigao del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SIARGAO, SIBUYAN.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 9 November 2025

species.citation: Wight , Icon. Pl. Ind. Orient. (Wight) 4 (1850) t.1601 ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 384. = Oldenlandia brachyphylla Merr. , Fl. Manila (1912) 448; Type: Merrill Philip. Pl. 705 (US, poss. iso; not cited in the original publication). ≠ Mitrasacme alsinoides auct. non R.Br. ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 311.
species.distribution: Australia, China, India, Japan, Java, Korea, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: NCR.
species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (28-Sep-19): See Gibbons et al. 2015 for notes about the name Mitrasacme prolifera R.Br. Old rice paddies and open wet grasslands (Merrill 1912). 0-500m.

species.citation: Domin , Biblioth. Bot. 22 (1928) 517 ; Gibbons et al., Telopea 18 (2015) 499; Type: from Australia. ≠ Mitrasacme pygmaea auct. non R.Br. ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 382.

R.Br. , Prodr. Fl. Nov. Holland. (1810) 453 ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 382. = Mitrasacme nudicaulis Reinw. ex Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 849 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 311. ≠ Mitrasacme alsinoides auct. non R.Br. ; Merr., PJS 1 (1906) Suppl. 116. var. pygmaea. Distribution: Australia, Cambodia, China, India, Japan, Java, Korea, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province, Rizal. Native. var. malaccensis (Wight) H.Hara, J. Jap. Bot. 30 (1955) 23; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 383; ≡ Mitrasacme malaccensis Wight, Icon. Pl. Ind. Orient. (Wight) 4 (1849) t.1601. ≠ Mitrasacme polymorpha auct. non R.Br.; C.B.Clarke in Hook.f., Fl. Brit. India 4 (1883) 80; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 311. Distribution: Australia, Borneo, China, Indochina, Japan, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand. LUZON: Bataan, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Mountain Province, Rizal. Native.
species.distribution: LUZON: Abra (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Mountain Province, Rizal, MINDORO.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 5 July 2025

species.citation: (J.F.Gmel.) Torr. & A.Gray , Fl. N. Amer. (Torr. & A. Gray) 2 (1846) 45 ; Merrill, PJS 11c (1917) 307; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 311; ≡ Cynoctonum petiolatum J.F.Gmel. , Syst. Nat., 13th Ed. (1791) 443. = Cynoctonum mitreola (L.) Britton , Mem. Torrey Bot. Club 5 (1894) 258 ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 375; ≡ Ophiorrhiza mitreola L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 150.
species.distribution: America, Australia, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BOHOL, LUZON: Pangasinan, Rizal, MINDORO.
species.notes: Bound to areas subject to a dry season. Fl. at the end of the rainy season. 0-600m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 5 July 2025

species.citation: (Koord.) Leenh. , Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 6 (1962) 367; ≡ Couthovia celebica Koord. , Meded. Lands Plantentuin 19 (1898) 537 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 313.
species.distribution: Moluccas, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi. MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, Surigao, MINDORO.
species.notes: In both dry and swampy sites, along riverbanks. Fl. mainly Jan-May; fr. Mar, Jul. 0-400m.

species.citation: Gardner , Hooker's J. Bot. Kew Gard. Misc. 1 (1849) 327 ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 341; Wong & Sugau, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 2 (1996) 221; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 11. = Norrisia philippinensis Elmer , LPB 4 (1912) 1482 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 311; Type: Elmer 12058, Sibuyan: Magallanes, Mt Giting-Giting, Mar-1910.
species.distribution: Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. DINAGAT (photos), HOMONHON, MINDANAO: Surigao del Norte (photos), Surigao del Sur (photos), SAMAR, SIBUYAN.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 27 July 2024

species.distribution: America. MINDANAO: Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao del Norte (photos).
species.notes: Open to semi-open abandoned farmlands and along road leading to forest interiors (M.G.Q. Rule, pers. comm., 10-Nov-20).

species.citation: Benth. , J. Proc. Linn. Soc., Bot. 1 (1856) 102. ≠ Strychnos nux-vomica auct. non L. ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 349, only as to Philippine specimens.
species.distribution: China, Hong Kong, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam. MINDORO.
species.notes: Along the coast above tide level. Fr. Apr. Once collected in the Philippines in Puerto Galera Bay.

species.citation: Colebr. , Trans. Linn. Soc. London, 1st Ser. 12 (1819) 356 ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 358; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 11. = Strychnos cenabrei Merr. , PJS 20 (1922) 433 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 312. = Strychnos impressinervis A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1917 (1917) 180 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 312. = Strychnos wenzelii Merr. , PJS 11c (1916) 202 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 313. = Strychnos tesseroidea A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1917 (1917) 206 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 313.
species.distribution: Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BALABAC, DINAGAT (photos), HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON, PALAWAN, SAMAR, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TAWI-TAWI.

species.citation: P.J.Bergius , Mater. Med. (Bergius) 1 (1778) 146 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 312; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 347; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 11; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1194.
species.distribution: Borneo, China, Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Vietnam. BILIRAN, HOMONHON, LEYTE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao del Norte, Lanao del Sur, Surigao, SAMAR.
species.notes: Sea level and lowland dipterocarp or mixed forests, sometimes along riverbanks. Fl. Jan-Sep; fr. Jan-Dec. Fruits reportedly eaten by monkeys and civet cats. Lowland to 1500m.

species.citation: A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1911 (1911) 299 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 312; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 355.
species.distribution: Cambodia, Philippines, Thailand. MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Lanao del Sur.

species.citation: Elmer , LPB 1 (1908) 332 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 312; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 358.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Quezon.
species.notes: Known only from the type. Humid forest. Fl. May. Elmer 15571 cited by Elmer (1908) and Merrill (1923), and Elmer 8251 cited by Merrill (1923) are Strychnos minor. 500m.

species.citation: Dennst. , Schlüssel Hortus Malab. (1818) 33. = Strychnos dubia A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1912 (1912) 298 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 312. = Strychnos merrillii A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1911 (1911) 297 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 312. = Strychnos multiflora Benth. , J. Proc. Linn. Soc., Bot. 1 (1857) 102 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 312. = Strychnos similis A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1912 (1912) 38 ; Kew Bull. (1917) 153; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 313. ≠ Strychnos colubrina auct. non L. ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 355.
species.distribution: Australia, Borneo, India, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Vietnam. APULIT, CULION, LUZON: Albay, Apayao, Benguet, Bulacan, Camarines Sur, Cavite, Ilocos Norte (photos), Isabela, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Surigao del Norte, Surigao del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PAMALICAN, POLILLO, SAMAR.

species.citation: A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1917 (1917) 156 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 313; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 353.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Sorsogon, PALAWAN.

species.citation: A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1909 (1909) 360 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 313; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 351. = Strychnos panayensis A.W.Hill , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1917 (1917) 148 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 313.
species.distribution: Borneo, China, Indochina, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. APULIT, BABUYAN ISLS, BICAWAYAN, CALAMIANES, DALUPIRI, LUZON: Laguna, Quezon, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY, TICAO.
species.notes: Lowland thickets and forests, along riverbanks. Fl. May-Jun; fr. Feb, May. 0-250m.
